records.ety: [L. cohaerere, cohaesum; co- + haerere to stick, adhere. See Aghast, a.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Cohered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Cohering (?).]

defs: To stick together; to cleave; to be united; to hold fast, as parts of the same mass.
quotes: Neither knows he . . . how the solid parts of the body are united or cohere together.

defs: To be united or connected together in subordination to one purpose; to follow naturally and logically, as the parts of a discourse, or as arguments in a train of reasoning; to be logically consistent.
quotes: They have been inserted where they best seemed to cohere.

defs: To suit; to agree; to fit.
quotes: Had time cohered with place, or place with wishing.
